title: 5 Dishonour and Return of Cheques Procedures
body_text:
5	Dishonour and Return of Cheques Procedures
5.1	Returning Cheques
Use ORS to dishonour and return cheques. Refer to the Online Return System (ORS) Part for procedures. If your financial institution is unable to return an item in ORS, you can enter a manual return. See Chapter 8, Entering Manual Returns and Reimbursement Requests on ServiceNow.
If you return an item through the Clearing System in contravention of CPA Rules, your financial institution is responsible for reimbursing any interest lost to the Negotiating Financial Institution.
Encoding differences of $20 or less are absorbed by your financial institution.
5.2	Missing Items
A missing item is an Incoming Clearing Item that is drawn on your financial institution, but an image isnГўВҖВҷt available. Since the CPAГўВҖВҷs time frames for return come into effect once your financial institution can make the pay/no pay decision, your financial institution isnГўВҖВҷt required to make this decision until receiving an image of the missing item. There are two options for handling missing items:
Request a copy of the item from Central 1. Once a copy of the item is received, you can make the pay/no pay decision. Create a ServiceNow Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request to request a copy of a missing item. Refer to Chapter 8, Entering Manual Returns and Reimbursement Requests on ServiceNow, for procedures on creating a ServiceNow ticket.
Return the item immediately. Proceed as follows:
Qualified Item
ГўВҖВ“ Return the item via ORS and select the applicable return reason code from the drop-down list. Only a substitute image displaying ГўВҖВңimage unavailableГўВҖВқ will appear on ORS.
Unqualified Item
ГўВҖВ“ Return the item manually. See Chapter 8, Entering Manual Returns and Reimbursement Requests on ServiceNow.
5.3	Returning Cheques with Forged Endorsement
A cheque can be returned for the reason ГўВҖВңforged endorsementГўВҖВқ up to and including six years after receipt by the Drawee. If a Payee contends that their endorsement on a cheque paid by your financial institution has been forged, the Payee must fill out a statutory declaration.
Proceed as follows to return a cheque for reason ГўВҖВңforged endorsementГўВҖВқ:
Obtain a statutory declaration from the Payee. See CPA Rule A4, Appendix III, Declaration that the Endorsement of a Payment Item is a Forgery.
Note:	Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can use Statutory Declaration ГўВҖВ“ Unauthorized Cheque Transactions, Form 3759.
Return the cheque to the Negotiating Financial Institution via ORS. Select
Forged Endorsement
as the reason for return. See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for procedures.
Retain the declaration for 12 months.
Related Information
Statutory Declaration ГўВҖВ“ Unauthorized Cheque Transactions,
Form 3759
(available to Operations Manual Program subscribers only)
5.4	Returning Cheques for Reason Intended Payee not Paid
A cheque can be returned for the reason ГўВҖВңintended Payee not paidГўВҖВқ up to and including six years from the date that it is cleared. If a cheque has a missing or incomplete endorsement, and the Payee donГўВҖВҷt receive or authorize anyone to receive funds, the Payee must sign a statutory declaration.
Proceed as follows to return a cheque for reason ГўВҖВңintended Payee not paidГўВҖВқ:
Obtain a statutory declaration from the Payee. See CPA Rule A4, Declaration that the Named and Intended Payee(s) donГўВҖВҷt Receive the Funds.
Note:	Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can use Statutory Declaration ГўВҖВ“ Intended Payee Not Paid, Form 2518.
Return the cheque to the Negotiating Financial Institution via ORS. Select
Intended Payee not Paid
as the reason for return. See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for procedures.
Retain the declaration for 12 months.
Related Information
Statutory Declaration ГўВҖВ“ Intended Payee Not Paid,
Form 2518
(available to Operations Manual Program subscribers only)
5.5	Returning Cheques for Reason Material Alteration
A cheque can be returned for the reason ГўВҖВңmaterial alterationГўВҖВқ up to and including 90 calendar days after being received by the Drawee. A cheque is materially altered if an unauthorized change is made to one or more of the chequeГўВҖВҷs details (e.g., the amount, Payee name, Drawee name, date).
Proceed as follows to return a cheque for reason ГўВҖВңmaterial alterationГўВҖВқ:
If the alteration is detected after one business day, and within 90 calendar days after being received by the Drawee, proceed as follows:
Obtain a statutory declaration from the Payee. No declaration is necessary for alterations detected within one business day. See CPA Rule A4, Appendix V, Declaration form for a Material Alteration.
Note:	Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can use Statutory Declaration ГўВҖВ“ Unauthorized Cheque Transactions, Form 3759.
Return the cheque to the Negotiating Financial Institution via ORS. Select
Material Alteration
as the reason for return. See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for procedures.
If applicable, retain the declaration for 12 months.
Related Information
Statutory Declaration ГўВҖВ“ Unauthorized Cheque Transactions,
Form 3759
(available to Operations Manual Program subscribers only)
5.6	Returning Cheques with a Forged DrawerГўВҖВҷs Signature
Normally, the signatory on a cheque (the Drawer) wonГўВҖВҷt discover that their signature has been forged until after the cheque has been paid. If more than one business day has passed since your financial institution received the cheque as an Incoming Clearing Item, the item
canГўВҖВҷt
be returned to the Negotiating Financial Institution. Instead, it will likely represent a potential loss to your financial institution. If your financial institution is advised of the forgery within one business day of receipt, the cheque can be returned to the Negotiating Financial Institution via ORS for the reason ГўВҖВңsignature forged/irregularГўВҖВқ. A statutory declaration isnГўВҖВҷt required.
If a cheque with a forged DrawerГўВҖВҷs signature canГўВҖВҷt be returned, it may be eligible for claim under your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs insurance coverage (Master Bond in BC; CUMIS in Ontario and Atlantic region; or your financial institution bond insurer). Central 1 requires your financial institution to obtain and submit a statutory declaration with all forgery claims. We recommend that you establish an internal policy to reflect this requirement.
5.7	Dishonouring Money Orders
The procedures and time frames that apply to dishonouring and returning cheques also apply to money orders. However, because money orders are prepaid instruments, they canГўВҖВҷt be returned for the following reasons:
Non-sufficient funds
Stale-dated
Funds not cleared
Payment stopped
If a bank draft or money order is lost or stolen before issue, use the return reason ГўВҖВңOther ГўВҖВ“ Lost or Stolen prior to IssueГўВҖВқ.
Note:	In some instances, money orders arenГўВҖВҷt drawn as negotiable instruments, and s. 145 of the
Bills of Exchange Act
, which pertains to material alteration, may apply. If you arenГўВҖВҷt sure if a money order can be returned for reason ГўВҖВңmaterial alterationГўВҖВқ, it is recommended that you contact your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs corporate solicitor.
5.8	Dishonouring Paper Pre-authorized Debits (PADs)
While most Pre-authorized Debits (PADs) are processed electronically, there are some that are exchanged in a paper format. These paper PADs are identified by the word ГўВҖВңPADГўВҖВқ on the face of the item. Paper PADs are treated as cheques and can be returned electronically, via ORS. They are returned to the designated branch of return (i.e., the branch that originally entered the PAD into the clearing) and are generally subject to the same time frames for return as cheques.
The following table outlines the reasons and time frames that a PAD can be returned:
PAD Type
Reason
Return Time Frame
Personal
The PAD wasnГўВҖВҷt drawn in accordance with the PayorГўВҖВҷs PAD Agreement
The PayorГўВҖВҷs PAD Agreement was revoked
Notification of the PAD wasnГўВҖВҷt given in accordance with the CPA Rules
PayorГўВҖВҷs PAD Agreement doesnГўВҖВҷt exist between the Payor and Payee
90 calendar days
Funds Transfer
90 calendar days
Business
10 business days
Cash Management
PayorГўВҖВҷs PAD Agreement doesnГўВҖВҷt exist between the Payor and Payee
90 calendar days
Your customer must complete a Reimbursement Claim for a PAD, Form 1701. Your financial institution must retain a copy of the form and provide it to the PayeeГўВҖВҷs financial institution upon request. See the AFT Returns and AFT Notice of Change Transactions Part for procedures on how to complete a Reimbursement Claim for a PAD, Form 1701, and handle a disputed PAD.
Related Information
Reimbursement Claim for a PAD,
Form 1701
AFT Returns and AFT Notice of Change Transactions Part
END
